ground_truth.title: 5 Best Value & Cheapest Project Management Software
ground_truth.author: Mehak Siddiqui
ground_truth.main_content:
You don't need to spend a ton of money to organize your projects and boost productivity. We've found the cheapest project management software that provide the best value, with robust features to track multiple tasks and help teams of all sizes collaborate. In this article, we compare the top budget-friendly project management software options to help you find one that best fits your needs. We assess each tool's features, ease of use and overall value to ensure you're getting your money's worth.

Cloudwards' Choice: nTask - I believe nTask is the best affordable project management software because its entry-level Premium plan (starting at $3 per user per month, annually) includes powerful features like custom fields, Gantt charts, bulk actions and task dependencies. Moreover, even the free version includes native time-tracking and issue-tracking tools, plus core task management and team collaboration features.

How to Choose: In addition to pricing, we considered key functionalities that help different kinds of teams meet their objectives: core task management features (task creation, progress tracking, kanban boards, timelines), collaboration tools (comments, file sharing, team chat), automations and integrations, ease of use, and security and permissions.

The Top 5: nTask ($3/month), ClickUp ($7/month), Trello ($5/month), monday.com ($12/month), and Asana ($10.99/month).

nTask is our top choice because it offers essential task management and collaboration features on a budget. Even the free version of nTask offers native time-tracking and issue management tools, which are usually available only on paid plans. However, it lacks built-in automations, instead relying on integrations with other apps.

ClickUp includes a wide array of features on its budget plan, including customizable views, real-time collaboration and limited automations and dashboards. It also has a generous free plan that supports unlimited users. However, its expansive feature set can feel complex, so it involves a bit of a learning curve to master.

Trello is a user-friendly option for teams that prefer kanban boards. It's the simplest tool on this list, making it ideal for personal use or small teams.

monday.com is a highly visual, user-friendly and versatile project management platform. Major brands like Coca-Cola, McDonald's and Hulu are part of its customer base.

Asana is an adaptable tool suitable for personal projects or complex resource management, featuring a polished, color-coded interface.
with: I believe nTask is the best affordable project management software because its entry-level Premium plan (starting at $3 per user per month, annually) includes powerful features like custom fields, Gantt charts, bulk actions and task dependencies.
nTask offers built-in issue and risk tracking: These features are typically included on higher-tier plans only, but nTask makes them available on its entry-level free plan.
ClickUp features more than 15 project views: Choose between list, board, calendar, Gantt chart or mind map views, with some limited usage available even on the free plan.
Core task management features form the backbone of project management software and include task creation, progress tracking and views like kanban boards and timelines.
nTask Premium plan, starting at just $3 per user per month, is the best deal — it includes features like Gantt charts, kanban boards, bulk actions, subtasks, task dependencies and budget tracking.
without: Facts checked by Igor Kurtz (Fact-checking editor)
